properties.detail: 60-day congressional authorization deadline under War Powers Act. Senate Republicans defeated WPR for 5th time (52-47) on Apr 23. Legal debate: does ceasefire pause the clock? If not paused, Trump needs Congressional authorization by May 1 or faces legal challenge.

Joint Pentagon press conference. Hegseth: 'shoot to destroy' mine-layers, Iran navy = 'gang of pirates with a flag', blockade 'as long as it takes'. Caine: 'persistent maritime dominance over the strait.' Hegseth also criticizes EU/Asia for 'freeriding' (Bloomberg).

Araghchi arrives Islamabad Friday night with small delegation. IRNA: 'bilateral' visit. May travel to Muscat then Moscow. Possible US meeting Monday if conditions met. Iran denies direct US talks planned.

White House confirms Witkoff/Kushner Saturday departure for Pakistan. 2nd round Iran talks 'intermediated by Pakistanis.' Vance on standby. Leavitt says format mirrors round 1. Araghchi already in Islamabad. Possible US-Iran meeting Monday.

properties.cause: Hormuz blockade 'as long as it takes' + Ghalibaf uncertainty + IRGC threats; pullback on Witkoff/Kushner departure news

properties.detail: Deadliest day since ceasefire began Apr 17. 6 Hezbollah fighters killed in Bint Jbeil airstrike, 2 killed in Touline airstrike, Hezbollah fires rockets at Shtula. Total 8 killed. UN calls for investigation. Hezbollah lawmaker calls extension 'meaningless.' Both sides accuse other of violations.

Senate Republicans defeat War Powers Resolution for the 5th time. Democrats unable to force withdrawal timeline. May 1 marks 60-day deadline. Legal debate over whether ceasefire pauses the constitutional clock. Sen. Kaine leads opposition effort.
